summary::Marvel's Blade is not canceled,: Marvel's Blade is not canceled, Arkane dev confirms after Summer Game Fest no-show and over two years of silence. The game was in development for several years before its absence from the Summer Game Fest event, which has sparked rumors that it may be cancelled or delayed. However, an official statement from Arkane Studios has confirmed that Marvel's Blade is still on track to release.
summary::Crimson Desert's free updates a: Pearl Abyss's lead says Crimson Desert's free updates are a form of "free marketing" that will continue as long as there is demand for them, suggesting the game's ongoing support and development.
summary::Deltarune Chapter 5 is coming o: Deltarune Chapter 5 is coming out this month, just over a year after Chapters 3 and 4. This marks the fifth installment in the popular video game series, which has gained a dedicated fan base for its unique blend of RPG elements and quirky humor. The game's development team continues to refine the gameplay mechanics and storylines, ensuring that Chapter 5 meets the high standards set by previous installments.
summary::Dragon's Dogma 2 getting Ninten: Dragon's Dogma 2 is getting a permanent discount on its base game starting June 24th, with certain DLC items being discontinued. The first update will arrive tomorrow, June 10th, which may include enhancements to the gameplay experience of the base game. Additionally, Capcom has announced that Dragon's Dogma 2: Dark Arisen, an expansion for the original game, is coming out on Nintendo Switch 2 later this year, with a PC, PS5, and Xbox Series X/S versions also receiving the DLC on October 9th.
summary::Xenoblade Chronicles JRPG trilo: The Xenoblade Chronicles JRPG trilogy is getting Nintendo Switch 2 edition releases this year, with the first one available today. The new editions include updated graphics and improved performance, making them a great option for players looking to experience the series on the latest console. For developers, these updates will likely be of interest as they can help improve game optimization and performance.
summary::10 Questions I Have About The O: Link's appearance in the trailer was not given any clear indication of how he would be styled or if there would be significant changes to his design. The game's overall style and level of detail are still unknown. A faithful remake with updated controls and tech is likely a possibility, but it's unclear what this entails. The presence of new areas and story elements is also uncertain.
summary::A List Of Weird Little Guys In: Weird little guys like Bongo Bongo, Sheikah torture victims Ganondorf, and the occasional creepy monster populate Hyrule in The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time. These characters are often comical, conniving or evil, but also have a sort of aura about them that makes them stand out. Despite being a classic game released nearly 30 years ago, Nintendo is still experimenting with its Weird Little Guy formula in the remake, which raises concerns that some of these characters may be toned down in the new version.
summary::Everything We Noticed In The Ne: The new Kingdom Hearts IV trailer reveals that the story in the game will involve characters old and new, including some obscure ones. The hooded figure explains that they were transported to a new world called Quadratum, where darkness-an animating force can be wielded by both heroes and villains. This new world is more realistic than previous ones in the series, with Disney lands potentially visited through a dreamlike trance or computer interface. Characters such as Sora, Donald, Goofy, Luxord, Strelitzia, Vali, and Xehanort are also introduced in the trailer.
summary::Kingdom Hearts AI art accusatio: Kingdom Hearts AI art accusations hit Square Enix, with many questioning the quality of the artwork. The studio is facing criticism for its use of AI-generated images in the game's latest releases. This development has sparked concerns among developers who value high-quality visuals and are worried about the impact on their own projects.
summary::Solo Leveling season 3 finally: Solo Leveling season 3 finally gets release window and our worst fears have come true. The highly anticipated game has been delayed multiple times, with many fans fearing it would never arrive on time. Despite this, the latest update reveals that the release date is still set for some time in the future.
summary::The Odyssey Trojan Horse popcor: The Odyssey Trojan Horse popcorn bucket is a work of art and I want to see Christopher Nolan holding one. The unique design of the bucket, which features a curved shape reminiscent of ancient Greek pottery, has sparked widespread attention online. As a developer audience, understanding the technology behind this design could provide valuable insights into modern materials science and manufacturing techniques.
summary::10 Questions I Have About The Z: The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time remake has been announced for release on the Nintendo Switch in later this year, leaving many questions unanswered. The trailer provided no clear answers to several key queries, including what Link looks like in action and whether the game will include significant changes from the original. A faithful remake or a total overhaul are also uncertain, with some speculating that it may be a mix of both. Additionally, players are left wondering if they'll have to relive the same challenges as before, such as navigating through Jabu-Jabu's stomach and carrying Ruto around.
